top_signals.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws
top_signals.summary: Microsoft Corp. today released software updates to plug at least 570 security holes in its Windows operating systems and other software, almost triple the number of vulnerabilities the software giant fixed in its record-smashing Patch Tuesday release last month. Microsoft attributed the burgeoning patch counts to vulnerability discoveries aided by artificial intelligence.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Before the Next Mythos Moment: The Case for an AI Threat Fusion Center
top_signals.summary:
Earlier this spring, Claude’s developers found the cyber potential of Anthropic’s model, Mythos 5, hazardous enough to pump the brakes on model release, creating the Project Glasswing collaboration to give cyber defenders the upper hand through controlled access to the model. Despite these efforts, the U.S. government stepped in on June 12, 2026, and forced Anthropic to take down Mythos and its safeguarded form, Fable 5 — an action the company disputed, calling the trigger a narrow, nonuniversal jailbreak. A lack of standard processes, complex legal terrain, and information-sharing obstacles resulted in a “pickup game” where, absent a shared factual

The post Before the Next Mythos Moment: The Case for an AI Threat Fusion Center appeared first on War on the Rocks.

top_signals.title: Franco-German defense cooperation under strain as Macron, Merz meet
top_signals.summary: The meetings come weeks after industrial rivalries forced Paris and Berlin to scrap the landmark Future Combat Air System (FCAS) fighter jet program.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Gaming Bonds Lose Allure as Threat From Prediction Markets Grows
top_signals.summary: Investors increasingly see gaming bonds as a losing bet.

top_signals.title: Hoisington, US Bond Bull for Decades, Turns Decidedly Bearish
top_signals.summary: Hoisington Investment Management Co., the bond manager known for its bullish stance on US Treasuries going back more than 30 years, has turned bearish.

top_signals.title: Polygon Labs announces second round of layoffs in 2026 as firm looks to finalize Coinme acquisition
top_signals.summary: "Two rounds of changes in one year is a lot to ask of a team, and I understand it is hard to manage," Polygon Labs CEO Marc Boiron said.

top_signals.title: 23andMe to pay $18 million in new genetics data breach settlement
top_signals.summary: Genetic testing company 23andMe has agreed to pay $18 million to settle claims from a coalition of 43 attorneys general that it failed to protect customers' genetic data. [...]
